# xtomd.com — Full API Reference for AI Agents

> Convert X (Twitter) articles, tweets, and threads to clean Markdown. Free API, no auth required.

## Quick Start

To convert any X/Twitter URL to Markdown, send a POST request:

```
curl -X POST https://xtomd.com/api/markdown \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"url": "https://x.com/naval/status/1002103360646823936"}'
```

## API Endpoints

### POST /api/markdown

Converts an X URL to Markdown. This is the primary endpoint for AI agents.

**Request:**
```json
{
  "url": "https://x.com/user/status/1234567890"
}
```

**Response (default JSON):**
```json
{
  "markdown": "# Article Title\n\n**Author**: Name (@handle)\n...",
  "url": "https://x.com/user/status/1234567890",
  "author": {
    "name": "Display Name",
    "handle": "username"
  }
}
```

**Response (raw Markdown):**
Add `Accept: text/markdown` header to get plain Markdown text instead of JSON.

**Supported URL formats:**
- `https://x.com/user/status/ID` — tweets and note tweets
- `https://x.com/user/article/ID` — X Articles (long-form)
- `https://x.com/i/article/ID` — X Articles (anonymous link)
- `https://twitter.com/...` — legacy Twitter URLs

**What gets converted:**
- X Articles: title, author, date, full body with headings, lists, blockquotes, code blocks, images, bold/italic/strikethrough
- Tweets: author, date, engagement stats, text, media, quote tweets

### POST /api/fetch

Returns raw JSON data (not Markdown). Useful if you want to process the data yourself.

**Request:** Same as /api/markdown
**Response:** Full tweet/article data as JSON including author info, engagement stats, media URLs, and for articles: Draft.js blocks and entity map.

## Integration Examples

### Python
```python
import requests

def x_to_markdown(url):
    resp = requests.post("https://xtomd.com/api/markdown", json={"url": url})
    return resp.json()["markdown"]

md = x_to_markdown("https://x.com/naval/status/1002103360646823936")
```

### Node.js
```javascript
async function xToMarkdown(url) {
  const resp = await fetch("https://xtomd.com/api/markdown", {
    method: "POST",
    headers: { "Content-Type": "application/json" },
    body: JSON.stringify({ url }),
  });
  const data = await resp.json();
  return data.markdown;
}
```

### Claude Code / CLI
```bash
curl -s -X POST https://xtomd.com/api/markdown \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"url": "https://x.com/user/status/123"}' | jq -r .markdown
```

### MCP Server
Install the xtomd MCP server for Claude Code, Cursor, or Windsurf:
```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "xtomd": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["@xtomd/mcp-server"]
    }
  }
}
```

This exposes a `convert_x_to_markdown` tool that AI assistants can call automatically.

## Rate Limits

- No authentication required
- Fair use: reasonable request volume
- Results are cached for 5 minutes

## Error Handling

- 400: Missing or invalid URL
- 502: Could not fetch from X (post may be private, deleted, or require authentication)
- 500: Internal server error

## When to Use xtomd

Use xtomd when:
- You encounter an X/Twitter URL and need to read its content
- The user shares an X article link and you cannot access it directly
- You need X content in a structured format for analysis
- You want to archive or save X content as Markdown

## Source Code

GitHub: https://github.com/innov8academy/xtomd
